briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for March 12, 2026.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Accelerating Domestic Climate Change Response Policies and Technological Innovation
sections.content: Domestically, active efforts in policy and technology development for climate change response are underway. The Ministry of Science and ICT has launched the 2nd Basic Plan for Climate Change Response Technology Development Promotion Committee, concretizing a future technology roadmap [1], and the Korea Institute of Ocean Science and Technology (KIOST) is leading climate crisis response through marine climate prediction research [6]. Furthermore, the legal foundation has been strengthened with the passage of six bills, including an amendment to the Carbon Neutrality Framework Act, by the National Assembly [8], and the Korea Environment Corporation is contributing to ESG disclosure compliance and the establishment of carbon neutrality data infrastructure by developing an AI-based carbon emission management system [9]. Concurrently, private sector participation is expanding, with iM Financial Group publicly demonstrating its excellent capabilities in climate change response at the CDP Korea Awards [7].

sections.title: 🌱 Widespread Expansion of Carbon Neutrality Practices and Renewable Energy Transition Efforts
sections.content: Concrete efforts to achieve carbon neutrality are spreading across the nation. Seongbuk-gu is selecting 'Gilrajabi' (guides) for carbon neutrality practice [10], and Geumsan-gun is recruiting carbon-zero practice stores [12], encouraging community participation. Notably, Changwon Leports Park achieved a tangible result by reducing 15.3 tons of carbon within two months of implementing the 'Nubija Carbon Neutrality Point System' [13], and Daejeon City is also recruiting participants for its vehicle carbon neutrality point system [16]. In the education sector, the Jeonnam Provincial Office of Education is accelerating the fostering of eco-citizens by selecting 50 carbon-neutral leading schools by 2026 [17], and the industry's eco-friendly transition efforts are continuing, with Hyundai E&C expanding carbon-neutral and green bonds [18]. Furthermore, with the introduction of 'direct trading' for solar power and research institutions presenting energy transition models [31], expectations for expanding renewable energy supply are rising.

sections.title: ⚠️ Escalating Climate Crisis Warnings and Increased Vulnerability
sections.content: Concurrently, alarm bells signaling the severity of the climate crisis are growing louder worldwide. According to one study, the possibility of accelerating global warming has been suggested [41], and a shocking research finding that the Earth's rotation is slowing down at an unprecedented rate in 3.6 million years has also been published [45]. Domestically, a phenomenon where seniors are confined to their homes for 900 hours annually during heatwave seasons is occurring [4], and the overall vulnerability of society is being exposed, such as questions being raised about the sustainability of the pear industry due to climate change [2]. Experts emphasize the need for immediate action, pointing out that climate change is extending allergy seasons [42] and even affecting the emotional well-being of adolescents [52].

sections.title: 🌍 Global Climate Response Trends and Challenges
sections.content: The international community is facing complex challenges alongside various attempts to address climate change. While LG Innotek has set an example for global corporations by achieving 'Leadership A' in the CDP Climate Change assessment for three consecutive years [40], legislation to protect workers from extreme weather is being reintroduced in Colorado [46]. However, China has set new carbon targets that test the balance between GDP growth and climate action [53], and concerns have been raised that the European Union's (EU) 2040 emissions target contains 'loopholes' [56], potentially delaying substantial climate action. Furthermore, while the advancement of AI technology holds the potential to contribute to achieving carbon neutrality goals [20], the anticipated tenfold increase in AI power demand is emerging as a new challenge [20].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted active climate action efforts both domestically and internationally, alongside escalating warnings of the climate crisis and emerging challenges. Global cooperation and sustained attention to address climate change will become even more crucial. This concludes today's climate briefing.
